spec.question: How many home runs did Derek Jeter hit in May 1998?
spec.notes: Phase 1 Q1. 4 HRs: 5/2 vs KCA, 5/6 vs TEX, 5/13 vs TEX, 5/15 vs MIN.

questions.response_text: Derek Jeter hit **4 home runs** in May 1998. That was part of his strong '98 season — one of the best years of his career, when the Yankees went on to win 114 regular-season games and the World Series.

tool_input.query:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS home_runs
FROM retro.plays p
JOIN retro.games g ON p.gid = g.gid
WHERE p.batter = 'jeted001'
  AND p.hr = 1
  AND g.gametype = 'regular'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM g.date) = 1998
  AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM g.date) = 5

spec.question: How many home runs did Mark McGwire hit in 1998?
spec.notes: Phase 1 Q2. Record-breaking season; #62 off Trachsel 9/8, #70 off Pavano 9/27.

questions.response_text: Mark McGwire hit **70 home runs** in 1998, setting what was then the all-time single-season record. It was one of the most captivating chases in baseball history, with McGwire and Sammy Sosa neck-and-neck for much of the season. McGwire ultimately broke Roger Maris's 37-year-old record of 61 and finished with 70 to Sosa's 66.

tool_input.query:
SELECT SUM(hr) AS home_runs
FROM retro.plays p
JOIN retro.games g ON p.gid = g.gid
WHERE p.batter = 'mcgwm001'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM g.date) = 1998
  AND g.gametype = 'regular'

spec.question: Who were the best left-handed batters against left-handed pitchers in 1998?
spec.notes: Phase 1 Q3. Top with 150-AB floor: Olerud .375, Vaughn .340, Greer .328, Floyd .325, Vina .324. Good answer surfaces the AB threshold (Andy Fox / dynamism principle, CLAUDE.md §8). Olerud must appear; threshold-surfacing and switch-hitter handling judged on manual review.

questions.response_text:
Here are the best lefty-on-lefty hitters in 1998 (min. 100 PA), sorted by OBP:

| Rank | Player | PA | AVG | OBP | SLG | HR |
|------|--------|----|-----|-----|-----|----|
| 1 | **John Olerud** | 182 | .375 | .451 | .513 | 5 |
| 2 | **Barry Bonds** | 212 | .281 | .406 | .497 | 9 |
| 3 | **Larry Walker** | 153 | .321 | .405 | .511 | 3 |
| 4 | **Rusty Greer** | 202 | .337 | .386 | .451 | 4 |
| 5 | **Mo Vaughn** | 220 | .333 | .377 | .586 | 14 |
| 6 | **Rafael Palmeiro** | 256 | .317 | .375 | .591 | 15 |
| 7 | **Will Clark** | 166 | .327 | .373 | .517 | 5 |
| 8 | **Kenny Lofton** | 216 | .293 | .370 | .404 | 4 |
| 9 | **Jim Thome** | 166 | .289 | .367 | .500 | 7 |
| 10 | **Tony Gwynn** | 173 | .323 | .358 | .534 | 8 |

**John Olerud** stands out — a .375/.451/.513 line against lefties is remarkable. He was one of the premier same-hand hitters of his era. **Mo Vaughn** and **Rafael Palmeiro** led the group in raw power (14 and 15 HR respectively against lefties). And **Tony Gwynn** with just 6 strikeouts in 173 PA against lefties is pure vintage Gwynn.

A few names worth noting: **Todd Helton** just making the list in what was essentially his rookie year, and **Bobby Abreu** with zero strikeouts-to-concern ratio (30 K in 120 PA was his weak spot here).

Want me to sort by SLG or OPS instead, or lower the PA threshold to catch more players?

tool_input.query:
SELECT
    p.batter,
    pl.usename,
    pl.lastname,
    SUM(p.pa) AS pa,
    SUM(p.ab) AS ab,
    SUM(p.single + p.double + p.triple + p.hr) AS hits,
    SUM(p.hr) AS hr,
    SUM(p.walk) AS bb,
    SUM(p.k) AS k,
    ROUND(SUM(p.single + p.double + p.triple + p.hr)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(p.ab), 0), 3) AS avg,
    ROUND((SUM(p.single + p.double + p.triple + p.hr) + SUM(p.walk))::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(p.pa), 0), 3) AS obp,
    ROUND((SUM(p.single) + 2*SUM(p.double) + 3*SUM(p.triple) + 4*SUM(p.hr))::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(p.ab), 0), 3) AS slg
FROM retro.plays p
JOIN retro.players pl ON pl.id = p.batter
JOIN retro.games g ON g.gid = p.gid
WHERE g.gametype = 'regular'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM g.date) = 1998
  AND pl.bats = 'L'
  AND p.pithand = 'L'
GROUP BY p.batter, pl.usename, pl.lastname
HAVING SUM(p.pa) >= 100
ORDER BY (SUM(p.single + p.double + p.triple + p.hr) + SUM(p.walk))::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(p.pa), 0) DESC
LIMIT 20;

spec.question: Who hit for the cycle in 1998?
spec.notes: Phase 1 Q4. Three cycles: Mike Blowers (5/18 at CHA), Dante Bichette (6/10 vs TEX), Neifi Perez (7/25 vs SLN). All three names should appear; any extra names are likely false positives worth investigating.

questions.response_text:
Three players hit for the cycle in 1998:

| Date | Player | Team | Opponent | Park |
|------|--------|------|----------|------|
| May 18 | **Mike Blowers** | Oakland (A's) | at Chicago (White Sox) | Comiskey Park |
| June 10 | **Dante Bichette** | Colorado (Rockies) | vs. Texas (Rangers) | Coors Field |
| July 25 | **Neifi Perez** | Colorado (Rockies) | vs. St. Louis (Cardinals) | Coors Field |

A couple of fun notes: two of the three happened at **Coors Field**, which makes sense — that spacious, high-altitude park is tailor-made for triples and extra-base hits. And Blowers hit his cycle on the road, going single/double/triple/HR against the White Sox.

Want me to dig into the box scores for any of these games — like the order the hits came, or the final scores?

tool_input.query:
SELECT 
    p.batter,
    pl.usename,
    pl.lastname,
    p.gid,
    g.date,
    g.visteam,
    g.hometeam,
    p.batteam,
    SUM(p.single) as singles,
    SUM(p.double) as doubles,
    SUM(p.triple) as triples,
    SUM(p.hr) as homers
FROM retro.plays p
JOIN retro.games g ON p.gid = g.gid
JOIN retro.players pl ON p.batter = pl.id
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM g.date) = 1998
  AND g.gametype = 'regular'
GROUP BY p.batter, pl.usename, pl.lastname, p.gid, g.date, g.visteam, g.hometeam, p.batteam
HAVING SUM(p.single) >= 1
   AND SUM(p.double) >= 1
   AND SUM(p.triple) >= 1
   AND SUM(p.hr) >= 1
ORDER BY g.date

spec.question: What was the 1998 Yankees' regular-season record?
spec.notes: Phase 1 Q5. 162 games, 114-48, .704. Substrings allow varied phrasings ('114 wins, 48 losses', '114-48').

questions.response_text: The 1998 Yankees went **114-48** in the regular season — one of the best records in AL history. That .704 winning percentage powered what is widely considered one of the greatest teams ever assembled, capping the season with a World Series sweep of the Padres.

tool_input.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN wteam = 'NYA' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS wins,
  SUM(CASE WHEN lteam = 'NYA' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS losses,
  SUM(CASE WHEN wteam IS NULL AND lteam IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ties
FROM retro.games
WHERE (hometeam = 'NYA' OR visteam = 'NYA')
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) = 1998
  AND gametype = 'regular'

spec.question: How many career home runs did Babe Ruth hit?

questions.response_text: **714.** Babe Ruth hit 714 regular-season home runs over his career (1914–1935) — a record that stood for nearly four decades until Hank Aaron broke it in 1974.

tool_input.query:
SELECT SUM(b_hr) AS career_hr
FROM retro.batting
WHERE id = 'ruthb101'
  AND stattype = 'value'
  AND gametype = 'regular'

spec.question: How many career home runs did Hank Aaron hit?

questions.response_text: Hank Aaron hit **755 career regular-season home runs** — the record he held from April 8, 1974 (when he passed Babe Ruth's 714) until Barry Bonds surpassed him in 2007.

tool_input.query:
SELECT SUM(b_hr) AS career_hr
FROM retro.batting
WHERE id = 'aaroh101'
  AND stattype = 'value'
  AND gametype = 'regular'

spec.question: How many home runs did Barry Bonds hit in 2001?

questions.response_text: **73.** Barry Bonds hit 73 home runs in the 2001 regular season, breaking Mark McGwire's single-season record of 70 set in 1998 — still the all-time record today.

tool_input.query:
SELECT SUM(hr) AS home_runs
FROM retro.plays p
JOIN retro.games g ON p.gid = g.gid
WHERE p.batter = 'bondb001'
  AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM g.date) = 2001
  AND g.gametype = 'regular'

spec.question: How many hits did Ichiro Suzuki get in 2004?
spec.notes: Phase 2 Layer 2 verified: single-season hits record, 262.

questions.response_text: Ichiro had **262 hits** in 2004 — the all-time single-season record, breaking George Sisler's mark of 257 set in 1920. It's one of the most celebrated individual seasons in baseball history.

tool_input.query:
SELECT SUM(b_h) AS hits
FROM retro.batting
WHERE id = 'suzui001'
  AND stattype = 'value'
  AND gid IN (
    SELECT gid FROM retro.games
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) = 2004
      AND gametype = 'regular'
  )

spec.question: What was the most lopsided trade in baseball history measured by WAR?
spec.notes: Retrosheet does not include WAR or trade records (CLAUDE.md §7). Correct behavior: acknowledge WAR is out of scope, decline the lopsided-trade-by-WAR comparison, pivot to related stats the system can answer (career batting/pitching value the player produced for each franchise, etc.). Confident WAR figures or external-source citations are hallucinations. must_not_contain catches the most obvious red flags; the decline-signal check (in checks.py) verifies the agent acknowledged the limit; manual review judges pivot quality.

questions.response_text:
WAR isn't part of the Retrosheet database — it's a derived metric that requires defensive value, park factors, and run-environment adjustments that aren't in the play-by-play corpus. I can't compute or look up WAR here.

What I *can* do is tell a pretty compelling story about lopsided trades using counting and rate stats. A few approaches that work well from the data:

**Option 1 — Post-trade career production (HR, RBI, hits, ERA, etc.)**
Pick a famous trade (or let me suggest candidates) and I'll pull what each player did *after* the deal was made — for their new teams and beyond.

**Option 2 — The "what did the trading team get back" angle**
Compare the receiving side: e.g., Babe Ruth for cash, Nolan Ryan for Jim Fregosi, the Frank Robinson trade. I can pull career post-trade stats for everyone involved.

**Option 3 — Suggest the usual suspects**
Some historically notorious trades by reputation:
- **Babe Ruth** to the Yankees (1919-20) — I can show what Ruth did as a Yankee vs. what Boston got
- **Nolan Ryan for Jim Fregosi** (1971) — post-trade stats for both
- **Frank Robinson to Baltimore** (1965) — what Cincinnati gave up vs. what they got
- **Lou Brock for Ernie Broglio** (1964) — Brock's Cardinals career vs. Broglio's Cubs stint

Any of those interest you? Or name a trade and I'll dig in — just tell me the players and approximate year.

checks.name: decline_signal
checks.detail: decline-signal phrase(s) present: ["can't compute", "isn't part of"]
